title: campium gaboonense
intro: Campium gaboonense, also known as African Stinging Nettle, is a tropical plant species native to Central and West Africa, characterized by its stinging hairs and broad leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Campium gaboonense include reducing pain and inflammation, treating skin conditions, and acting as a natural antiseptic to prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a poultice or tea, and has also been used in traditional medicine to treat snake and insect bites.
